  • Changelog

Package index

Package

sitemix sitemix-package
sitemix: Site- and group-level proportions, rates, and sampling uncertainty

Estimate site-year rates

Main entry points: student rows, sufficient counts, and published aggregates. Use sm_estimate() for the unified dispatcher; the wrappers preconfigure subsets of the same signature.

sm_estimate()
Estimate site-year rates and standard errors
sm_estimate_from_counts()
Estimate site-year rates from sufficient counts
sm_estimate_from_aggregates()
Estimate site-year rates from published aggregate rows

Audit uncertainty outputs

Diagnose scalar and covariance uncertainty and audit publisher-side suppression before downstream use.

sm_diagnose()
Diagnose uncertainty in a sitemix_estimates tibble
sm_suppression_report()
Audit aggregate-input suppression and accountability tiers

Inspect and stress-test covariance

Work with sm_vcov matrices directly; compute formal raw pairwise Fréchet intervals and separate projected stress scenarios for unidentified D1 aggregate dependence.

sm_vcov()
Construct or inspect a within-site covariance object
sm_frechet_envelope()
Compute D1 pairwise Fréchet intervals and projected stress scenarios

Smooth standard errors (experimental)

Opt-in generalized variance-function/log-variance sensitivity alternatives. A fixed-seed audit found no evidence for default promotion; append-only output preserves canonical SE columns and does not guarantee improvement.

sm_smooth_variance()
Smooth standard errors with an experimental GVF model

Reshape published aggregates

Pivot subgroup files into the schema expected by sm_estimate_from_aggregates(). Framing X turns subgroups into sites; Framing Y turns them into indicators.

sm_pivot_subgroups_to_sites()
Pivot subgroup aggregate rows into subgroup-as-site input (Framing X)
sm_pivot_subgroups_to_indicators()
Pivot subgroup aggregate rows into subgroup-as-indicator input (Framing Y)

Data

Bundled example data for documentation, examples, and tests.

alprek_subset
Alabama Pre-K sample panel

On this page
